title: Right Livelihood
justification: This talk provides the most comprehensive and coherent treatment of Right Livelihood, covering the Buddha's specific prohibitions, the importance of attitude and service in work, practical guidance for daily life integration, and rich personal anecdotes (the cake factory, the angry neighbor, the job applicant). At over 6,400 words, it offers substantial depth with excellent literary flow, moving from doctrinal foundations through practical application to meditation integration, making it ideal as a chapter spine.
summary: Bhante Bodhidhamma explores Right Livelihood within the Eightfold Path, explaining which trades to avoid and emphasizing that the spiritual quality of work lies not in what we do but in our attitude of service. He offers practical guidance on maintaining mindfulness at work, handling difficult colleagues with equanimity, and integrating meditation practice into daily professional life.

relevance.note: Entirely dedicated to sammā ājīva as fifth Eightfold Path factor
